name: s4hana-brownfield-vs-greenfield-playbook
description: Brownfield-vs-greenfield S/4HANA call for mid-size retail, plus the reusable assessment kit
body:
For a mid-size retail <kunde> with a clean-enough core, default to phased brownfield — recommend greenfield only when the ECC customisation debt is genuinely unrecoverable. The decision criteria and the reusable migration-assessment kit live at <path>. Owner: Marco Reinhardt (SAP Core Business Services). On the last two retail engagements the brownfield path roughly halved the cutover window versus a greenfield rebuild.

Related: [[sap-commerce-integration-expert]], [[transformation-estimation-heuristics]]

name: transformation-estimation-heuristics
description: Day-rate ranges and contingency heuristics for scoping end-to-end transformation engagements
body:
Captured from the Q1 delivery-leads workshop (Teams). For end-to-end transformation scoping: carry 20–25% contingency on any engagement that crosses three or more practice areas, and size discovery at ~10% of total effort. Replatforming a commerce frontend onto <commerce-platform> ran 15–20% over first-pass estimates on recent work — pad accordingly. Full rate card and worked examples at <path>. Owner: Anika Reddy (Strategy & Transformation).

Related: [[s4hana-brownfield-vs-greenfield-playbook]], [[engagement-retro-process]]

name: cra-gap-assessment-template
description: Where the CRA (EU Cyber Resilience Act) gap-assessment template lives and who owns it
body:
The CRA gap-assessment template for <kunde> engagements is at <path>. Owner: Maya Singh (CISO office, Transformation & Security). Use it before quoting any CRA-readiness work. Never paste real <secret> or <host> values into the assessment — placeholders only.

Related: [[nis2-scoping-checklist]]

name: nis2-scoping-checklist
description: Checklist for deciding whether a <kunde> engagement falls under NIS2
body:
Run the NIS2 scoping checklist at <path> before scoping any compliance engagement. If sector classification is unclear, ask Maya Singh (CISO office) — do not guess essential- vs important-entity status, it changes the obligations and the price.

Related: [[cra-gap-assessment-template]]

name: commerce-ux-research-kit
description: Reusable UX discovery & research kit for commerce/CX engagements
body: For commerce/CX discovery, start from the valantic UX research kit at <path> (interview guide, journey-map template, heuristic-eval checklist) instead of building from scratch — saves roughly 3 days on a typical discovery. Owner: Priya Shah (Customer Experience).

name: data-platform-reference-architecture
description: valantic reference architecture / accelerator for a client data & AI platform
body:
Default starting point for a <kunde> data platform: the valantic reference architecture at <path> (medallion layout, a governance baseline, and a RAG-ready serving layer). Pull in the Data & AI team during scoping so the platform actually fits the downstream AI use cases instead of being retrofitted. Owner: Sofia Lang (Data & AI).

Related: [[transformation-estimation-heuristics]]

name: sap-commerce-integration-expert
description: Internal go-to expert for SAP order-management ↔ commerce-frontend integration
body:
For SAP order-management ↔ <commerce-platform> integration questions, the go-to is Marco Reinhardt (SAP Core). He owns the integration-pattern library at <path>. Book him early on retail commerce engagements — the order-sync design drives the cutover plan.

Related: [[s4hana-brownfield-vs-greenfield-playbook]]

name: engagement-retro-process
description: How and where valantic captures lessons learned after each engagement so the brain stays fed
body:
Every engagement ends with a 30-minute retro. The reusable lessons — gotchas, estimates that were wrong, assets worth keeping — get written into the company brain by whichever assistant the consultant already uses (Claude, Copilot, ChatGPT, Vally), tagged with the practice area. Retro template at <path>. Owner: Anika Reddy (Strategy & Transformation). This is what keeps the brain current instead of letting knowledge die in a project channel.

Related: [[transformation-estimation-heuristics]]
